machine learning for data science.question: Why do Data Scientists use machine learning?
What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
What is the difference between classification and regression?
What is clustering, and when is it useful?
What is overfitting?
What is underfitting?
Why should data be divided into training and testing sets?
What is feature importance?
Why is model interpretability important?
Why shouldn't accuracy be the only evaluation metric?
How would you select an appropriate machine learning algorithm for a business problem?
How would you determine whether a machine learning model is solving the intended business problem?
Compare classification, regression, and clustering from a business perspective.
How would you explain a machine learning model's predictions to non-technical stakeholders?
How would you identify the most influential features affecting model predictions?
How would you compare two models with similar performance but different complexity?
How would you evaluate whether collecting more data is likely to improve model performance?
How would you determine whether machine learning is even necessary for a particular business problem?
Explain how feature engineering can improve model performance.
How would you communicate model limitations to business stakeholders?
Design a complete machine learning workflow for solving a customer churn prediction problem.
How would you balance prediction accuracy, interpretability, fairness, and business value when selecting a model?
How would you determine whether improvements in model accuracy produce meaningful business value?
Explain the trade-offs between simple interpretable models and highly complex machine learning models.
How would you evaluate whether a deployed model should be retrained?
How would you design experiments to compare multiple machine learning models while avoiding evaluation bias?
Describe strategies for improving model performance when additional training data is unavailable.
How would you evaluate whether feature importance remains stable over time?
Explain how business objectives influence machine learning model selection.
Design a framework for evaluating machine learning solutions from both technical and business perspectives.
A stakeholder requests a highly accurate model, but business leaders require explanations for every prediction. Explain how you would choose an appropriate solution.
A customer churn prediction model achieves excellent accuracy but fails to reduce actual churn. Walk me through your investigation.
A product manager wants to use machine learning for a problem that could easily be solved using simple business rules. How would you evaluate the proposal?
A newly trained model performs significantly better during testing but disappoints after deployment. Explain your investigation process.
Two machine learning models achieve similar technical performance, but one is much easier for stakeholders to understand. Describe how you would decide which model to recommend.
Your analysis shows that the most accurate model relies heavily on sensitive customer information. Explain how you would evaluate the ethical and business implications.
A business executive asks why the machine learning model made an unexpected prediction for an important customer. Describe how you would investigate and explain the result.
A marketing team requests customer segmentation for a new campaign. Explain how you would determine whether clustering is an appropriate solution.
A deployed recommendation model improves click-through rate but reduces long-term customer retention. Explain how you would evaluate overall business success.

You're responsible for developing machine learning solutions across multiple business departments. Explain your framework for problem definition, model selection, evaluation, stakeholder communication, deployment recommendations, and continuous improvement.
